channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: At least 38 killed as gunmen open fire on vehicles carrying Shiites in northwest Pakistan
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Gunmen opened fire on vehicles carrying Shiite Muslims in Pakistan's restive northwest on Thursday, killing at least 38 people, including six women, and wounding 20 others in one of the region's deadliest such attacks in recent years, police said.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: ICC seeks to arrest Israeli and Hamas leaders as Gaza war deaths top 44,000
item.description: The International Criminal Court issued arrest warrants on Thursday for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, his former defense minister and a Hamas military leader, accusing them of war crimes and crimes against humanity.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Australian teen and British woman who drank tainted alcohol in Laos have died, bringing toll to 5
item.description: VIENTIANE, Laos (AP) — An Australian teenager and a British woman have died after drinking tainted alcohol in Laos in what Australia’s prime minister on Thursday said was every parent’s nightmare.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US citizen among 4 dead in Laos after suspected alcohol poisoning
item.description: Four people have died after drinking tainted alcohol in Laos after officials said that multiple people had been sickened in town popular with backpackers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Treasury imposes sanctions on Russian banks, financial officials
item.description: Russia’s third-largest bank, Gazprombank, and its subsidiaries were hit with U.S. sanctions Thursday, making it the largest bank not already targeted as the Russia-Ukraine war nears the three-year mark.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Police in Finland arrest five suspects over separatist violence in southeast Nigeria
item.description: HELSINKI, Finland (AP) — Police in Finland said Thursday they had detained five suspects in connection with deadly violence in southeastern Nigeria and were seeking a court extension of the detentions.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The biggest remaining unsanctioned Russian bank hit with U.S. sanctions, nearly three years into war
item.description: Russia’s third largest bank, Gazprombank and its six foreign subsidiaries were hit with U.S. sanctions on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein And Dave Mchugh, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A former staffer exposes how Russia's disinformation machine worked in Central African Republic
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — When Ephrem Yalike-Ngonzo was first approached in 2019 by a Russian who suggested he help promote the activities of the Central African Republic's army and Russian forces in the country, the journalist believed that he was doing the right thing.
item.dc:creator: MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia fired ICBM at Ukraine, Kyiv says
item.description: Russian forces launched an intercontinental ballistic missile (ICBM) at Ukraine, according to Ukrainian officials in the military.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: International Criminal Court issues arrest warrants for Netanyahu, Hamas leader
item.description: The International Criminal Court (ICC) has issued arrest warrants for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Mohammed Diab Ibrahim Al-Masri, a Hamas leader commonly known as Mohammed Deif who is presumed dead, according to separate press releases.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Death toll in Gaza from Israel-Hamas war passes 44,000, Palestinian officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The death toll in the Gaza Strip from the 13-month-old war between Israel and Hamas has surpassed 44,000, local health officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ex-UK Deputy Prime Minister John Prescott is hailed as a working-class hero after his death at 86
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British politician John Prescott, a pugnacious and personable former merchant seaman who rose to the post of deputy prime minister in Tony Blair's Labour government, has died at age 86. Prescott’s family announced his death on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 38 killed as gunmen open fire on vehicles carrying Shiites in northwest Pakistan
item.description: Pakistani police say gunmen have opened fire on vehicles carrying Shiite Muslims in the country's restive northwest, killing at least 38 people and wounding 20 others.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia launched intercontinental ballistic missile toward Ukraine, air force says
item.description: Russian troops launched an intercontinental ballistic missile from the Astrakhan region in an early morning attack on Ukraine on Thursday, according to Ukraine's Air Force.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 19 Senators back limiting offensive weapons to Israel in key vote
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday failed to pass three resolutions that would have limited offensive arms to Israel, but 19 senators supported the initiative, showing there is growing resistance to the Israeli war against Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: About 1,500 migrants form a new caravan in Mexico. Here's what it means
item.description: TAPACHULA, Mexico (AP) — About 1,500 migrants formed a new caravan Wednesday in southern Mexico, hoping to walk or catch rides to the U.S. border. The migrants are mainly from Central and South America.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Admiral: Middle East, Ukraine conflicts strain US air defense stocks
item.description: Conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine are adding a strain to the U.S. military’s ability to respond to China, a top U.S. admiral warns.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: The dark energy pushing our universe apart may not be what it seems, scientists say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Distant, ancient galaxies are giving scientists more hints that a mysterious force called dark energy may not be what they thought.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US vetoes UN resolution demanding a cease-fire in Gaza because there's no link to a hostage release
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United States on Wednesday vetoed a U.N. Security Council resolution demanding an immediate cease-fire in the war in Gaza because it was not linked to an immediate release of hostages taken captive by Hamas militants in Israel in October 2023.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli officials demand the right to strike Hezbollah under any cease-fire deal for Lebanon
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli officials demanded Wednesday the freedom to strike Lebanon's Hezbollah as part of any cease-fire deal, raising a potential complication as a top U.S. envoy was in the region attempting to clinch an agreement.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: US vetoes a Gaza cease-fire resolution
item.description: The United States vetoed a U.N. resolution demanding an immediate cease-fire in the Israel-Hamas war in Gaza, with the Americans saying they the resolution Wednesday was not linked to an immediate release of hostages still held by Hamas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine launches first UK storm shadow missiles at Russia
item.description: Ukraine for the first time fired British storm shadow missiles into Russia on Wednesday, according to reports from Bloomberg and Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: US vetoes UN resolution demanding Gaza cease-fire
item.description: The U.S. on Wednesday voted against a United Nations Security Council resolution that demanded an immediate cease-fire in Gaza, citing the lack of a path for releasing hostages held by Hamas as the reason. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Zelenskyy answers whether he's willing to cede Crimea, other territory in peace deal
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy tells Fox News that his country "cannot legally acknowledge any occupied territory of Ukraine as Russian.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Doctors Without Borders suspends operations in Haiti
item.description: Doctors Without Borders has suspended its activities in Port-au-Prince following a series of threats by Haiti's police forces, the organization announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Netanyahu offers $5 million to Palestinians for every Israeli hostage they help free
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu offers a $5 million reward for any Palestinian in Gaza who helps secure the freedom of Israeli hostages still held by Hamas after more than a year in captivity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian lawmakers endorse bill to ban adoptions by gender-transition countries
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russia’s upper house of parliament on Wednesday endorsed a bill banning adoption of Russian children by citizens of countries where gender transitioning is legal. The Federation Council also approved bills that outlaw the spread of material that encourages people not to have children.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US Embassy in Ukraine warns of 'significant air attack,' closes
item.description: The U.S. Embassy in Kyiv sounded the alarm on a "potential significant air attack" Wednesday, announcing that it will close. The warning also recommended American citizens in the area to prepare to shelter in place.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: US Embassy in Kyiv closed as 'potential significant air attack' looms
item.description: The U.S. Embassy in Kyiv, Ukraine, has released a warning stating that they have "specific information of a potential significant air attack" taking place on Wednesday, Nov. 20.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Son of Norway's crown princess arrested on suspicion of rape
item.description: Marius Borg Høiby, the eldest son of Norway Crown Princess Mette-Marit, was arrested on suspicion of rape in Oslo late last night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine fires several US-made longer-range missiles into Russia for the first time
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine fired several American-supplied longer-range missiles into Russia, officials said Tuesday, marking the first time Kyiv used the weapons that way in 1,000 days of war.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 1,000 days of war in Ukraine as Zelenskyy doubles down on aerial options with ATACMS, drones and missiles
item.description: Ukraine marks 1,000 days of war with Russia on Tuesday as President Zelenskyy eyes plan to develop thousands of long-range drones and military targets inside Russia using US-supplied ATACMS.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 45 pro-democracy Hong Kong activists sentenced to up to 10 years in prison under China-backed law
item.description: Western countries are outraged after Hong Kong used a Chinese national security law to sentence 45 pro-democracy activists to up to 10 years in prison.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: India overtakes China as top source of international students at US colleges
item.description: For the 2023-2024 school year, India saw a massive 23 percent increase in the number of college students sent to America.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Bolton: Moscow, Beijing should feel ‘a lot of relief’ if Gabbard is confirmed as DNI
item.description: Former national security adviser John Bolton said that Russia and China should feel “a lot of relief” if former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii) is confirmed as President-elect Trump’s choice for director of national intelligence. “She’s not capable of doing it,” Bolton said Sunday on CNN.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Russia launches one of its fiercest missile and drone attacks at Ukraine's infrastructure
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Russian strike on a nine-story building in the city of Sumy in northern Ukraine killed eight people and wounded dozens, an official said Sunday, as Russia launched a massive drone and missile attack described by officials as the largest in recent months.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and JOANNA KOZLOWSKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Montana Tucker's Oct. 7 film showcases young victims' strength
item.description: Montana Tucker's film, "The Children of October 7," documents what some of the attacks' youngest victims went through.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation
